title: styphelia tamminensis
intro: Styphelia tamminensis, also known as the Tammin Styphelia or Western Tammin Styphelia, is a shrub native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Styphelia tamminensis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Styphelia tamminensis include teas and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments that can be applied directly to the skin.
